query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "openrath: session-centered runtime state for agent systems" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (d) and (a) as it proposes a foundational "runtime" framework with explicit state management (safe defaults, underwriting boundaries, and action-level tolls) for autonomous AI agents, which directly parallels the "Session-Centered Runtime State" mechanism in the user's referenced work. It provides the canonical theoretical baseline for the specific domain of runtime state and safety guarantees in agent systems, making it essential prior work for a literature review
